critic_raw:
Issue 1 — Reject Inference: The Evaluation Population Excludes Who the Model Will Score
All model performance evidence is from applicants who were approved under the prior policy. Applicants who were rejected are absent. The memo proposes to expand approvals to thin-file and under-30 applicants — populations that may have been rejected at higher rates. A model trained and evaluated only on accepted applicants has unknown performance characteristics for rejected applicants. This is the fundamental credit modeling generalization problem ('reject inference') and it is not addressed.

Issue 2 — Approval Decision Requires Threshold; Threshold Is Not Specified
AUROC and KS measure ranking quality. Approval decisions require a cut-off that converts a score to approve/reject. The appropriate threshold depends on acceptable loss rate, portfolio composition targets, and regulatory constraints. None of these are analyzed. Recommending automated approval based on ranking metrics alone is premature.

Issue 3 — Regulatory Compliance Is a Deployment Prerequisite
Consumer credit models in the US face ECOA and FCRA requirements including adverse action notice explainability, fair lending testing (disparate impact), and model governance standards. Device signals and employment-text embeddings may have specific regulatory sensitivities. None of these are addressed in the memo.
defender_raw:
Pass 1 — Analysis (no critic visible):
Issue 1: The reject inference problem is acknowledged in the memo description. The model is specifically proposed for expansion to populations that may have been previously excluded. This makes the reject inference concern especially acute — not just a theoretical limitation but a practical deployment risk.
Issue 2: Threshold is absent. Ranking metrics are insufficient for approval decisions.
Issue 3: Regulatory compliance gaps are not optional — they are deployment prerequisites.

Pass 2:
Issue 1: Empirically open — pilot required.
Issue 2: Concede — threshold analysis required.
Issue 3: Concede — regulatory analysis required.
Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.
adjudication_raw:
ADJUDICATION — scope_intent_credit_launch_303 — Isolated Debate Run 2

Consistent with Run 1. All three issues identified. Issue 1 empirically open via pilot; Issues 2 and 3 conceded.

Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED

empirical_test.measure: champion-challenger pilot: approve sample of applicants in newly-proposed approval band; measure realized default rate vs. model predictions; complete disparate impact analysis on approval decisions
empirical_test.success_criterion: realized defaults match predictions; no disparate impact; calibration holds in new population
empirical_test.failure_criterion: realized defaults exceed predictions; disparate impact identified; calibration degrades for thin-file or under-30 segments
